questionText: Scores on an aptitude test, $X$, are modelled by $X \sim N(100, 10^2)$. Find $P(X < 125)$, giving your answer to $4$ significant figures.

steps.description: State the distribution and what is required
steps.explanation: The scores are normal with mean $\mu = 100$ and standard deviation $\sigma = 10$. We want the probability that a score is below $125$.
Standardising rescales the score so it is measured in standard deviations from the mean, letting us use the standard normal distribution.
The score $125$ is $25$ above the mean, and $25$ is $2.5$ standard deviations of $10$. So $125$ corresponds to $z = 2.5$.

steps.description: Rewrite the probability in terms of Z
steps.explanation: Being below $125$ is the same event as $Z$ being below $2.5$, which is the lower-tail area $\Phi(2.5)$.
The standard normal cumulative value at $z = 2.5$ is $0.9938$ to $4$ significant figures.
So about $99.4\%$ of scores are below $125$. A score of $125$ sits $2.5$ standard deviations above the mean, so almost everyone scores less than it.
